untranslatable_reason: This signal is a meta-rule about trading philosophy (prioritizing fundamental analysis over technical correlation/consensus) rather than a mechanically translatable technical condition. It requires: (1) external fundamental thesis validation (not available in price/technical indicators), (2) consensus/crowd positioning measurement (not provided in allowed indicators), (3) logical consistency checking of thesis across time (requires qualitative judgment). The core directive—'reject coincidental correlations and trade sound fundamentals regardless of crowd'—cannot be encoded as technical indicator conditions without access to fundamental data, sentiment indicators (VIX is allowed but insufficient), or explicit correlation matrices between assets.
translation_notes: SCHWAGER_DRY_0_2 is a regime-context signal emphasizing trading discipline and thesis validation over pattern-matching. Tradeable implementation would require: (1) external fundamental scoring system, (2) sentiment/positioning data (e.g., COT reports, put/call ratios), (3) multi-timeframe thesis invalidation logic. Suggest implementing as a meta-filter overlaid on technical signals: reject any entry where the trade setup is based solely on recent correlation (measured via rolling correlation indicator, not in allowed set) without independent fundamental or technical justification. Parameters suggest full position sizing on sound theses and holding through crowd invalidation, but these are position management rules, not entry/exit conditions.
